figures.caption: Figure 6: Evolution of the training losses over iterations. Individual contributions from the PDE residual, the inner boundary condition (Γ), and the outer absorbing boundary condition (Γout) are shown separately.

Figure 9: Normalized pointwise error between the trained model predictions and the FEM reference solutions, as a function of the incidence angle. The vertical dashed lines at ±30◦ delimit the training range.

Figure 2: Schematic representation of the physics-informed DeepONet solving an Helmholtz problem. Highlight the decomposition of total wave field into the incident field Winc and the scattered field Ws
Figure 5: Symmetrical geometry encoded with ϕ evaluated in ten different points. It serves as input of the branch net.
Figure 8: Absolute pointwise error between the trained model predictions and the FEM reference solutions, for three incidence angles. The +60◦ case lies outside the training range, i.e., [−30◦,+30◦].
Figure 1: 2D Domain with inclusion and absorbing outer borders. Highlight the incident wave field Winc and the scattered field Ws
Figure 7: FEM solution of the Helmholtz problem (Incidence angle: 0◦)
